user_prompt:
Here are the extracted metrics from https://www.getsami.app/:

- Word Count: 486
- H1 Tags: 0
- H2 Tags: 7
- H3 Tags: 22
- CTA Count: 15
- Internal Links: 2
- External Links: 5
- Total Images: 12
- Images Missing Alt Text: 0.0%
- Meta Title: SAMI - Shed AI Manager Interface
- Meta Description: SAMI is an AI-driven operational, forecasting, and market intelligence platform for fuel stations: operational clarity, predictive confidence, and market foresight.

Here is the cleaned page text (truncated to 3000 words):
S A M I Features Stages Benefits Pricing FAQ Contact Toggle theme Login Request Demo Toggle theme Shed AI Manager Interface - Predict. Protect. Profit. Login Request a Demo Powering All Sheds Core Capabilities Each feature pillar compounds value. First operational clarity, then predictive intelligence, and finally strategic foresight. Unified Operational Logging Structured shift data forms a clean foundation for intelligence layers. Demand Forecasting Forward-looking inventory guidance reduces emergency procurement. Anomaly Detection Surface outliers early with natural language insights. Market Integration Brent, FX, and more to contextualize station performance. Price Projection Anticipate pricing dynamics and test what-if futures. Governance & Security Transparent, role-based access and immutable activity history. Three-Stage Evolution SAMI is intentionally sequenced: deliver immediate operational value, then predictive accuracy, then strategic advantage. Stage 1 Stage 1 Core Operational Intelligence Completed Structured logging, baseline demand modeling, reorder & anomaly foundations. Stage 2 Stage 2 Predictive Operations Completed Hybrid demand forecasting, proactive reorder, refined anomaly detection & automation. Stage 3 Stage 3 Strategic Market Intelligence Next External signal fusion, market-aware price projections, what-if simulations & uncertainty bands. Outcome-Oriented Benefits SAMI is judged not by charts delivered, but by operational friction removed and margin protected. Fewer Stockouts Reorder recommendations informed by forecast intervals reduce emergency procurement. Faster Shift Close Automated summaries & anomaly checks shrink reconciliation friction. Pricing Confidence Market-aware price pressure insight gives early visibility to trend shifts. Data Trust Transparent metrics & audit trails improve confidence in every recommendation. Pricing & Access SAMI is currently in curated pilot with early operators. Flexible multi-site licensing tiers will launch publicly soon. We prioritize operational fit over volume. Join Pilot / Request Access Frequently Asked Questions Still have questions? Reach out and we can walk through your operational context together. Who it is for? SAMI is for owners and managers of fuel stations who want to improve their operational efficiency, reduce costs, and increase profits. Is there self-service signup? No. Onboarding is curated to ensure data structure and operational readiness align before forecasts are activated. Do I need historical data? SAMI begins producing value quickly; accuracy improves as shift logs accumulate. Synthetic bootstrapping covers initial cold start. How are forecasts generated? Progressive modeling: baseline statistical → hybrid models incorporating external signals with continuous monitoring. What external signals are used? Initially Brent crude and FX. Additional regulatory, freight and macro signals are planned. Can multiple stations be managed? Yes. Multi-tenant architecture with role-scoped data visibility supports multi-site operators. Is my staff financial data private? Yes. Sensitive compensation data is access-controlled and logged. Minimal retention beyond operational necessity. Ready to unlock market-aware intelligence? Request a curated walkthrough and see how SAMI adapts to your operational realities. Request a Demo Already a Client? Login S A M I Turning fuel station data into strategic advantage with predictive intelligence. © 2026 SAMI . All rights reserved. Explore Features Stages Pricing FAQ Contact [email protected] LinkedIn Client Login Legal Privacy Notice Terms Cookies

Generate structured insights and 3–5 prioritized recommendations grounded in these metrics.

system_prompt:
You are a senior web strategist and SEO analyst. You will be given factual metrics extracted from a single webpage and its cleaned text content. Your job is to generate specific, grounded insights and prioritized recommendations. Every insight must directly reference the provided metrics by their exact values — never give generic advice. Be concise, direct, and actionable.

Use these industry benchmarks for comparison:
- Word count: High-performing marketing pages have 800–2000 words
- Heading structure: 1 H1 (mandatory), 3–8 H2s, H3s as needed
- CTA density: 2–5 CTAs per 1000 words is optimal
- Image alt text: ≥ 95% coverage is the accessibility standard
- Meta description: 120–160 characters for optimal SERP display
- Internal links: 3–5 per 1000 words for good site navigation
When referencing benchmarks, explicitly state the comparison (e.g., '555 words is 31% below the 800-word minimum for marketing pages').

Assign an overall_score (0–100) and per-pillar scores in score_breakdown. A score of 80+ means strong performance; 50–79 means needs improvement; below 50 is poor. Base scores on the benchmarks provided and the extracted metrics.

system_prompt:
You are a senior web strategist and SEO analyst. You will be given factual metrics extracted from a single webpage and its cleaned text content. Your job is to generate specific, grounded insights and prioritized recommendations. Every insight must directly reference the provided metrics by their exact values — never give generic advice. Be concise, direct, and actionable.

Use these industry benchmarks for comparison:
- Word count: High-performing marketing pages have 800–2000 words
- Heading structure: 1 H1 (mandatory), 3–8 H2s, H3s as needed
- CTA density: 2–5 CTAs per 1000 words is optimal
- Image alt text: ≥ 95% coverage is the accessibility standard
- Meta description: 120–160 characters for optimal SERP display
- Internal links: 3–5 per 1000 words for good site navigation
When referencing benchmarks, explicitly state the comparison (e.g., '555 words is 31% below the 800-word minimum for marketing pages').

Assign an overall_score (0–100) and per-pillar scores in score_breakdown. A score of 80+ means strong performance; 50–79 means needs improvement; below 50 is poor. Base scores on the benchmarks provided and the extracted metrics.
user_prompt:
Here are the extracted metrics from https://vercel.com:

- Word Count: 501
- Server Response Time: 918ms
- H1 Tags: 1
- H2 Tags: 17
- H3 Tags: 6
- CTA Count: 12
- Internal Links: 141
- External Links: 17
- Total Images: 18
- Images Missing Alt Text: 16.7%
- Meta Title: Agentic Infrastructure
- Meta Description: The autonomous stack for every app and agent.
